title: Podman 5.3.1 fails to start on M1 Macbook (Sonoma 14.7.1) - "vfkit exited unexpectedly"
body:
### Issue Description

Ran the following sequence of commands ([related issue](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21842#issuecomment-2147821775)):

```
$ brew install podman
$ podman machine reset
$ podman machine init
$ brew tap cfergeau/crc
$ brew reinstall vfkit
$ podman machine start --log-level debug
```

Debug log output attached, and it includes a `vfkit` usage error...

[podman-debug.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18084909/podman-debug.txt)

```
Usage:
  vfkit [flags]

Flags:
  -b, --bootloader strings      bootloader configuration (default [])
  -c, --cpus uint               number of virtual CPUs (default 1)
  -d, --device stringArray      devices
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. M1 Mac
2. Install podman & vfkit
3. podman machine start


### Describe the results you received

[podman-debug.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18084909/podman-debug.txt)

### Describe the results you expected

Working podman

### podman info output

```yaml
OS: darwin/amd64
provider: applehv
version: 5.3.1
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Looks like my Mac was incorrectly set up with a `x86_64` brew installation ([link](https://blog.podman.io/2024/03/the-dreaded-vfkit-exit-1/)), giving me the wrong Podman as a result.

```
$ brew config
HOMEBREW_VERSION: 4.4.11
macOS: 14.7.1-x86_64
Rosetta 2: true
```

Uninstalled and reinstalled ([instructions](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16262#issuecomment-1518425894)) to get `arm64` brew:

```
$ brew config
HOMEBREW_VERSION: 4.4.11
macOS: 14.7.1-arm64
Rosetta 2: false
```

And now all seems to be well:

```
$ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Machine "podman-machine-default" started successfully```
